topic: China SG null (2009) — verdict change Misleading→Self-Contradicted has landed
change_summary: verdict_changed bucket: WIN-014.verdict moved Misleading → Self-Contradicted in commit 51d98750 (workspace-sync 2026-05-06T18:05:08Z, applied via FUSE workspace). Companion fields all refreshed: finding 37→199 chars, detail_verdict_text 166→662, tldr_evidence 239→305, tldr_verdict 99→110, detail_evidence 4609→4640. Used fallback (§1f) — drift-audit.json (generated 2026-05-05T20:52Z) listed 5 OBE items at the head; OBE-skip cap of 3 forced the in-prompt fallback. Compared against c5 fingerprint stored 2026-05-04T10:42Z.
fallback_reason: OBE-skip cap of 3 exceeded — drift-audit.json head candidates WIN-050/028/009/036/060 all already re-reviewed since the audit ran
our_argument_summary: WIN-014 has been upgraded to Self-Contradicted (matching the c3/c4/c5 recommendation) and all surface fields (finding, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) now consistently express the self-contradiction structure: the dome claims a 2009 null SG result confirms the model AND simultaneously predicts a positive 10–13 µGal SG contact-phase pulse for the same instrument class at Aug 2026 (PRED-TIER-3) — whichever horn holds, the other fails. The c5 H13 dangling-fragment regression has been corrected (the prose now reads cleanly: "After tidal and atmospheric reduction, SG residuals of this generation reach the 0.1–1 µGal floor"). The c5 H3-PARTIAL hedge has been propagated in detail_evidence (the affirmative "no contact-phase anomaly was reported" is replaced with the publication-absence "no published English-language contact-phase residual analysis from the 2009 campaign has been located"). The c5 H14 cross-WIN sign-convention inconsistency is also resolved: WIN-012 detail_evidence now uses positive "10–13 µGal" in all 5 occurrences (verified by direct grep against data/wins.json:WIN-012.detail_evidence) and WIN-014 cites predictions.json as authoritative. Two issues persist as expected: H1 (SEC-8b.3 still enumerates E-PRED-A/B/C/D — yeeted to EXP-292 per the 2026-05-06 daily report; ISS-1825 status assigned-analyst) and H8 (eight-gravimeters provenance — ISS-1829 still open, minor). Three NEW issues for c6: (1) cross-WIN asymmetry with WIN-013, which has the structurally identical SG-null-vs-PRED-TIER-3 pattern but retains the older WIN-011-vs-WIN-013 framing and the "Misleading" verdict; (2) issue-tracker desync — ISS-1335 in open-issues.json is still pending-human with fix_date null even though the verdict change has actually landed; (3) numerical range "10×–100× above floor" is slightly understated against the strict ratio 13/0.1 = 130×.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py entry for WIN-014 — no domain, no fetch, no comparison. The WIN exists only as static prose in the published WIN list. The cross-link to PRED-TIER-3 (data/predictions.json entry "SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals") is verified against the entries array — id present, "what" field matches the magnitude cited in our review.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — no computation in monitor.py for WIN-014. The post_hoc/derives_from_dome tags are correctly set to true/false respectively.
data_sources_used: Sun et al., attributed 2010 (the dome's cited source — unverifiable in English-language indices); doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 (the 2013 closest-match Chinese Journal of Geophysics paper our review cites as the only English-locatable record)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The dome's likely escape — that 2009 null reflects Level-1/Level-2 reduction while PRED-TIER-3 specifies Level-3 residuals — is partially preempted by the burden-shift "the burden is on the dome to identify what processing step would hide it. No such identification has been offered." See advocate_mode for a sharper preemption.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — WIN-014.code_analysis correctly reports monitoring=none, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false, relabels_standard=false.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no WIN-014 entry, no domain class for "china_sg_2009" or similar, and no fetch logic for SG residual data. Verified by grep on raw-text/monitor.py for "014" and "china sg" — no matches in monitoring code paths. The WIN exists as a static prose claim only.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics does not specifically predict that a 2009 China SG eclipse measurement should produce a null — the prediction is "no anomaly above instrument noise" only as a default null hypothesis (no eclipse-coupling effect exists). The dome model claims the null specifically confirms its electromagnetic-not-gravitational interpretation. This is a positive interpretive claim, not a relabel of an existing standard-physics prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Sun et al. attributed 2010 (or the 2013 Chinese Journal of Geophysics paper at doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 — the only English-language publication our literature search located) predates the dome model's adoption of this WIN. The dome did not pre-register a "this campaign should show null" prediction in any timestamped venue independent of the author; the value was adopted from the literature.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome-geometry derivation produces "the 2009 China SG eclipse anomaly should be 0 µGal." The claim is that the null is consistent with an electromagnetic (not gravitational) coupling mechanism — that consistency does not derive a numerical prediction from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude / moon_altitude. The 0 µGal expectation is the standard-physics null, not a dome-derived numerical prediction.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN structural inconsistency now visible. WIN-014 has been upgraded to "Self-Contradicted" on the basis that (a) the dome claims 2009 China SG null confirms the model and (b) the dome's PRED-TIER-3 forward prediction specifies 10–13 µGal in the same SG instrument class — whichever horn holds, the other fails. WIN-013 (Membach SG, 1999 eclipse, Van Camp 2001) has the same structural pattern: a published SG null is claimed as confirmation, while the same PRED-TIER-3 prediction implies the SG instrument class will detect 10–13 µGal at C1/C4 contact phases. WIN-013's current detail_verdict_text frames the contradiction as WIN-011-positive vs WIN-013-null (instrumental hierarchy + spatial unfalsifiability) and retains the verdict "Misleading". A reader who lands on WIN-014 and clicks the inline "Similar to WIN-013" link encounters: WIN-014 = Self-Contradicted, WIN-013 = Misleading, both invoking the same SG instrument class. The asymmetry will be noticed and used to argue our verdict-assignment is inconsistent.
holes_found.recommendation: Two acceptable paths. (a) Upgrade WIN-013 to Self-Contradicted with a parallel framing that adds the SG-vs-PRED-TIER-3 dimension on top of the existing WIN-011-vs-WIN-013 unfalsifiability argument — its detail_evidence already contains the instrumental-hierarchy + electromagnetic-vs-gravitational lines that the c5 burden-shift uses, so the addition is incremental. (b) Add a clause to WIN-013 detail_evidence explicitly stating why the PRED-TIER-3 self-contradiction angle does NOT also apply at Membach (e.g., "Membach was outside the 1999 eclipse path of totality" or "PRED-TIER-3 specifies sites the dome model has pre-named, which Membach is not"). I lean toward (a) — the structural pattern is the same and the dome's PRED-TIER-3 is generic to the SG instrument class, not site-specific. If decider prefers (b), the analyst needs to verify the dome has actually constrained PRED-TIER-3 to specific sites; the entries array currently has no site list. Either way, opening a fresh issue (ISS-NEW for WIN-013) is appropriate.

SEC-8b.3 (data/sections.json:part8b) still enumerates E-PRED-A/B/C/D as live commitments (each appears once in the section JSON, verified by literal-string count). The contradiction is unchanged; the analyst is now actively working on EXP-292 to either (a) verify E-PRED-C presence with a fresh dome-page fetch, or (b) re-frame SEC-8b.3 with a date-stamped note pointing at the 2026-03-12 raw-text snapshot.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action from the curmudgeon side. Tracking ISS-1825/EXP-292 to its conclusion. When the analyst delivers the EXP, expect a follow-up queue push for adversarial review.

holes_found.description: ISS-1335 in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json still has status "pending-human", fix_date null, updated_at 2026-05-04T09:20:24Z. But the verdict change has actually landed in data/wins.json — first appearing in commit 51d98750 (workspace-sync 2026-05-06T18:05:08Z) — which means the human applied the patch directly to the FUSE workspace and synced through, rather than going via the decider's normal close-issue workflow. Net effect: the issue tracker no longer reflects the truth of the data files. A future audit comparing open-issues against wins.json verdicts will trip on this. (verified: grep ISS-1335 monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json returns no match; grep verdict in data/wins.json:WIN-014 returns "Self-Contradicted".)
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should close ISS-1335 on its next run with fix_details: "Verdict change applied directly via FUSE workspace by human; landed in commit 51d98750 (2026-05-06T18:05:08Z). All companion fields (finding, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) refreshed in the same patch — verified by curmudgeon c6." Move to closed-issues.json. This is housekeeping — no content change required.

holes_found.description: Detail_evidence's burden-shift ("the burden is on the dome to identify what processing step would hide it. No such identification has been offered") is correctly framed but does not preempt the most likely dome-side rebuttal. A defender will argue: "the 2009 published Sun-et-al data is Level-1/Level-2 reduction (broadband tidal+atmospheric removal), whereas PRED-TIER-3 specifies Level-3 residuals — narrow-band filtering centered on the contact-phase frequency, with a per-station reference subtraction. The 10–13 µGal pulse is sub-noise in broadband Level-2 (because the broadband floor in the contact-phase short-correlation regime is much higher than the long-period 0.1–1 µGal cited) and emerges only after Level-3 narrow-band processing. So 2009 null and 2026 positive prediction are not contradictory." This evasion has surface plausibility because it concedes our textual claim ("0.1–1 µGal floor at minute timescales") is the long-period broadband number, not the short-correlation Level-3 number. Our text already cites the 0.1–1 µGal range with the "after tidal and atmospheric reduction" qualifier — but does not explicitly close the loophole by demanding the dome specify the spectral/temporal property of the predicted pulse that distinguishes Level-2-floor from Level-3-floor. Without that closure, a sympathetic reader can grant the rebuttal.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_evidence after the existing burden-shift, before the "The dome cannot simultaneously claim" sentence. Suggested wording: "If the dome maintains that the contact-phase pulse appears only in Level-3 narrow-band residuals and not in standard broadband Level-2 reduction, the burden extends to specifying the carrier frequency, bandwidth, and coherence length of the predicted pulse that would make it sub-noise in broadband processing; no such spectral or temporal specification has been published in the dome's WIN texts, monitor.py, or predictions.json." This does not require a verdict revisit — the self-contradiction holds — but it preempts the most credible dome-side rebuttal.

holes_found.description: Detail_evidence states: "a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse exceeding this floor by 10×–100× would be reportable even at Level-2 reduction." Strict computation: floor 0.1–1 µGal, pulse 10–13 µGal. Ratios — minimum 10/1 = 10×; maximum 13/0.1 = 130×. The text's "10×–100×" is ~30% understated at the upper bound. This does not weaken the argument (any pulse 10×+ above floor is plainly reportable), and the "100×" wording is a natural rounded number. But a sharp dome-defender reader can flag the arithmetic and use it to seed a "your numerical claims are loose" narrative.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "10×–100×" with either "10×–130×" (strict) or "more than an order of magnitude (10×–130×)" (clearer for a non-numeric reader). One-character edit territory; not blocking.

holes_found.description: The c5 H3-PARTIAL hedge is correctly applied in detail_evidence (publication absence vs empirical absence), but tldr_evidence still uses the affirmative-absence framing: "The dome later predicted a positive 10–13 µGal SG pulse from the same instrument class (PRED-TIER-3) — the 2009 null refutes this forward claim." The phrase "the 2009 null refutes" is mildly stronger than the detail-level burden-shift wording. Doesn't mislead — the TLDR is for skim readers and the simplification is fine — but a precision-minded reader could read the TLDR as more confident than the detail. The hedge has not propagated 100%. Compare: detail_evidence already uses "no published English-language contact-phase residual analysis from the 2009 campaign has been located" (publication-grounded), but tldr says "the 2009 null refutes" (empirical-grounded).
holes_found.recommendation: If the TLDR is touched in a future patch, consider: "The dome later predicted a positive 10–13 µGal SG pulse from the same instrument class (PRED-TIER-3) — incompatible with the published 2009 null." This propagates the publication-grounded hedge and avoids the slightly stronger "refutes" verb. Not blocking; flagging as part of the H3-PARTIAL hedge propagation that c5 H12 anticipated.

holes_found.description: C3/C4/C5 H8 still open as ISS-1829 (open, minor). "Eight gravimeters were deployed across six sites in China for this eclipse" remains uncited in detail_evidence. No movement since c5. Carrying forward.
holes_found.recommendation: No new recommendation; ISS-1829 holds the original c3 fix proposal.

stronger_arguments.description: Pull WIN-013 into the same self-contradiction frame (see H15-NEW). If the analyst extends WIN-013's argument to add the PRED-TIER-3 dimension, the eclipse-gravity cluster becomes a tightly-coupled three-WIN self-contradiction (WIN-012 on the κ-derivation magnitude side; WIN-013 and WIN-014 on the SG-null-vs-positive-prediction side). The dome's own predictions.json entries become the load-bearing evidence — predictions the dome itself published, against the historical results the dome itself cites. This is rhetorically much stronger than the WIN-011-vs-WIN-013 unfalsifiability argument alone.

Cite Crossley et al. 2013 review of SG noise floors directly in the burden-shift sentence rather than the parenthetical. The current text places the citation as a parenthetical to the floor number, but the burden-shift sentence does not name a specific reference. A defender can read "the burden is on the dome" as bare assertion. Anchoring the burden-shift to Crossley et al. (or alternatively to the Global Geodynamics Project reduction protocols) gives the burden a documentary footing.
sources: Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013, Reports on Progress in Physics; Van Camp & Vauterin 2005

kernel_of_truth.description: Two genuine insights. First, superconducting gravimeters are indeed dramatically more sensitive than spring gravimeters at long periods, and this asymmetry would matter if the dome were claiming a small steady gravity perturbation rather than a transient contact-phase pulse. The dome could have built a coherent argument about why the WIN-011 Mohe spring-gravimeter signal is below SG-class detection threshold for some instrument-physics reason — and that argument would have legitimate technical content (cite data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence "instrumental hierarchy" paragraph). Second, the historical July 22 2009 eclipse was a genuine well-instrumented event with multiple SG deployments — the dome's framing of it as a meaningful test is not absurd on its face (cite the same detail_evidence paragraph). The contact-phase argument the dome makes (that the eclipse-coupling effect is concentrated at C1/C4 not totality) has at least face-validity given that contact phases are when the geometric configuration changes most rapidly.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel is undermined by the dome's own forward-prediction structure. By publishing PRED-TIER-3 (data/predictions.json entry: "SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals"), the dome commits to a positive 10–13 µGal SG signature — squarely above the SG instrument-class noise floor by a factor of 10 to 130 (cite WIN-014.detail_evidence "0.1–1 µGal floor (Van Camp, 2005; Crossley et al., 2013)"). That positive-pulse prediction is in the same instrument class as the 2009 null the dome claims as confirmation. The instrumental-hierarchy and contact-phase nuances that could rescue the WIN against a generic gravity-anomaly criticism do not rescue it against the dome's own published forward prediction in the same instrument class. The kernel-of-truth analysis would matter if the dome had not committed to PRED-TIER-3; with PRED-TIER-3 on the books, the dome itself cancels the rescue.

deeper_analysis: The verdict-change cycle (c3 recommend → c4 confirm → c5 hold-pending-human → c6 verify-landed) has now closed. The patch as landed is high-quality: not a bare verdict-string flip but a coordinated rewrite of finding (37→199 chars), detail_verdict_text (166→662), tldr_evidence (239→305), tldr_verdict (99→110), and propagation of the c5 H3-PARTIAL publication-grounded hedge into detail_evidence. The c5 H13 dangling-fragment regression is fixed. The c5 H14 cross-WIN sign-convention inconsistency is fixed (WIN-012 now uses positive 10–13 in 5 places). Two pre-existing issues persist as expected (H1/ISS-1825 → EXP-292 active; H8/ISS-1829 minor open). The new findings split between (a) cross-WIN propagation work that should follow naturally from the verdict change (H15: WIN-013 is now structurally inconsistent with WIN-014 unless extended), (b) housekeeping (H16: tracker-issue desync), (c) pre-emptive sharpening (H17: Level-2 vs Level-3 evasion not closed; H19: TLDR retains affirmative-absence wording the H3-PARTIAL hedge resolved at detail level), and (d) numerical tightening (H18: 10×–100× should be 10×–130×). None of these challenge the verdict — the verdict change is correct and now stands on coherent textual ground. The main strategic recommendation is H15 (extend the self-contradiction frame to WIN-013) because it converts the eclipse-gravity cluster into a three-WIN coordinated self-contradiction story rather than the current mixed two-WIN-Self-Contradicted + one-WIN-Misleading shape.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome-defender, having read our PRED-TIER-3 cross-reference, would argue: "the 2009 China SG campaign published Level-1/Level-2 reduction — broadband tidal-and-atmospheric removal optimized for the dominant tidal signal. PRED-TIER-3 specifies Level-3 residuals — narrow-band filtering with per-station reference subtraction at the contact-phase frequency. The 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse is sub-noise in broadband Level-2 (because the broadband short-correlation floor at minute timescales is order-of-magnitude higher than the long-period 0.1–1 µGal floor your review cites) and emerges only after Level-3 narrow-band processing centered on the contact-phase carrier. The 2009 null reflects what Level-2 reduction shows; PRED-TIER-3 predicts what Level-3 reduction will show. There is no self-contradiction — the two claims are about different processing depths." Furthermore, the defender could note that no published Level-3 contact-phase residual analysis from the 2009 campaign has been located (which our own text now states as a publication-absence) — so the dome could maintain the 2009 null is consistent with PRED-TIER-3 because the 2009 data was simply never processed at Level-3 depth.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_evidence after the existing burden-shift: "If the dome maintains that the contact-phase pulse appears only in Level-3 narrow-band residuals and not in standard broadband Level-2 reduction, the burden extends to specifying the carrier frequency, bandwidth, and coherence length of the predicted pulse that would make it sub-noise in broadband processing; no such spectral or temporal specification has been published in the dome's WIN texts, monitor.py, or predictions.json." (This is also recorded in H17-NEW.) The defense is then closed: the dome must either commit to the spectral specification (and accept the standard test against 2009 broadband data within whatever spectral window they specify) or accept the unfalsifiability finding.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-013 (Membach SG null, 1999 eclipse) is structurally identical to WIN-014 (China SG null, 2009 eclipse) for the purposes of the PRED-TIER-3 self-contradiction argument: both are SG-instrument-class nulls claimed as confirmation, both are subject to the dome's same forward 10–13 µGal SG-class prediction. After the c6 verdict change, WIN-014 = Self-Contradicted while WIN-013 = Misleading; a careful reader notices the asymmetry. WIN-012 = Self-Contradicted (compatible with WIN-014). WIN-011 = the positive-detection counterpart and uses a different framing (its own framing is appropriate given the different verdict structure). The structural mismatch is specifically WIN-013 vs WIN-014. Recommendation in H15-NEW.

claims_checked: SG residual floor: 0.1–1 µGal (Van Camp 2005, Crossley 2013) — broadly consistent with published SG noise budgets after tidal+atmospheric reduction
Predicted pulse: 10–13 µGal at C1/C4 contacts (PRED-TIER-3, PRED-TIER-2) — confirmed against data/predictions.json entries
Floor-multiple range: text says "10×–100×". Strict computation: min 10/1 = 10, max 13/0.1 = 130. Range is therefore 10×–130×, not 10×–100×.
Sign convention: text says positive 10–13 µGal; WIN-012 detail_evidence verified to contain 5 occurrences of "10–13" and 0 of "−10 to −13" — c5 H14 fix confirmed.
2009 eclipse date — July 22, 2009: this is the well-known total solar eclipse over China, longest-duration of the 21st century at maximum (~6 min 39 sec) — date matches the dome's claimed event
quantitative_verification.computations: Direct ratio computation in node: const min=10/1; const max=13/0.1; → 10 and 130. Sign-convention check via JS substring matching against data/wins.json:WIN-012.detail_evidence: 5 "10–13" matches, 0 "(−|-|−)10 to (−|-|−)?13" matches. Predictions.json verified by JSON.parse + entries array filter on id.includes("TIER-").
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The "10×–100×" wording in detail_evidence is approximately 30% understated against strict computation (correct upper bound is 130×). Recommendation in H18-NEW. No other numerical discrepancies found — all other quantitative claims (instrument noise floors, pulse magnitudes, eclipse date, instrument class) check out.
